title: Articles & Reflections
subtitle: Stories and reflections from Arunachala
meta_description: Articles and reflections on Sri Ramana Maharshi's teachings, the Ashtavakra Gita, Upadesa Saram, the role of the guru, and the practice of self-enquiry.
body_html:
Essays and reflections on the teachings of Ramana Maharshi, the saints of Arunachala, and the practice of self-enquiry.

The Guru in the Ashtavakra Gita: The One Who Shatters the Dream

The Ashtavakra Gita's radical non-dual teaching declares liberation is already your nature — yet its transmission requires an enlightened guru, a ripe disciple, and lifetimes of karmic purification. A look at the paradox at the heart of this supreme Vedantic scripture.

Role of the Guru in Spiritual Practice

The guru is the direct link to the divine and the Self, guiding the seeker along the most direct path toward awakening.

Who Are You, Really?

Ramana Maharshi said there is no broken self to fix, only a case of mistaken identity to see through. His Forty Verses on Reality, explained through simple stories — a cinema screen, a rope mistaken for a snake, an actor who forgot the play was over.

Upadesa Saram: Ramana Maharshi's Essence of Instruction

Ramana Maharshi's distilled wisdom on self-realization — all thirty verses with commentary for the seeker.

More articles are published regularly. Full posts are added from the editorial archive.
